Alpha Weapons.name: Tonans (vs Titan)
Alpha Weapons.notes:
VERY IMPORTANT: While Tonans beats Vendicatoire in terms of burst vs Titans at base, the Titan damage boost is additive with boosts from nuclear amplifier, etc
TLDR: Vendicatoire does ~15% more damage than Tonans when both have a fully stacked NA on a damage dealer titan.

Alpha Weapons.name: Furnace (Heat x2)
Alpha Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by each furnace when you have two furnaces, torches, or a combination of the two. Yes, they do less damage the more you have. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Alpha Weapons.name: Furnace (Heat x3)
Alpha Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by each furnace when you have three furnaces, torches, or a combination of the three. Yes, they do less damage the more you have. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Heavy Weapons.name: Liodari (Charged)
Heavy Weapons.notes:
A full charge requires enemies to deal about 350k damage to you. Shields count.
Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion.

Heavy Weapons.name: UE Incinerator
Heavy Weapons.notes: UE Incinerator gets about 90% of a blast per mag, so 90% of a blast is accounted for in the DPS. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Heavy Weapons.name: Liodari (Uncharged)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion.

Heavy Weapons.name: Basileus
Heavy Weapons.notes: Fragility effect can increase damage against enemies with defense points

Heavy Weapons.name: Hammer (25m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: One Hammer can get about half a blast per mag, so half a blast is accounted for in the DPS (assuming you're using 2+ Hammer)

Heavy Weapons.name: Pshent
Heavy Weapons.notes: If you start the fight with 2 orbitals prepared, your burst DPS will be double, but only for that initial burst.

Heavy Weapons.name: Kelvin
Heavy Weapons.notes: Assumes you stop shooting before they overheat, so the cooldown is only 5 seconds rather than 6 seconds.

Heavy Weapons.name: Boom
Heavy Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion. NO DEATHMARK BONUS

Heavy Weapons.name: Brisant (0-200m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: At 200m, it's still a slug shot, so the spread doesnt take any effect

Heavy Weapons.name: Piercer (Max Fatigue)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Assumes you're already at max fatigue stacks (5 stacks)

Heavy Weapons.name: Brisant (250m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: YMMV. Past 250m, the dispersion becomes dependant on target hitbox. Tested on Vityaz.

Heavy Weapons.name: Leinani (Machine Gun)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Includes the DoT damage, much of which is dealt while reloading.

Heavy Weapons.name: Screamer
Heavy Weapons.notes: Each sonicblast does 25k damage. A full mag of 1 screamer results in 1 blast. The blast is accounted for in the DPS. Spread at range not accounted for. 100% grey damage

Heavy Weapons.name: Hornet
Heavy Weapons.notes: Includes DoT damage, much of which is done while reloading

Heavy Weapons.name: Pilum
Heavy Weapons.notes: Pilum gets one third of a blast per mag, so one third of a blast is accounted for in the DPS.

Heavy Weapons.name: Nucleon (25m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: At close range, you dont need to let them cool off, so they can continually do their burst dps forever

Heavy Weapons.name: Nucleon (500m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: At longer range, nucleon needs to cool off about 75% of the way to max heat in order to hit every shot

Heavy Weapons.name: Hammer (150m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Hammer no longer reliably blasts at 150m, so no blast damage is accounted for in the DPS

Heavy Weapons.name: Cestus
Heavy Weapons.notes: One Cestus can get about half a blast per mag, so half a blast is accounted for in the DPS (assuming you're using 2+ Cestus)

Heavy Weapons.name: Talon
Heavy Weapons.notes: Fragility effect can increase damage against enemies with defense points

Heavy Weapons.name: Leinani (Energy Blaster)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Charges ships slowly, but concentrates damage in bursty energy balls that have small splash.

Heavy Weapons.name: Fengbao
Heavy Weapons.notes: Great titan chargerate but poor damage.

Heavy Weapons.name: Hammer (350m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Hammer no longer reliably blasts at 350m, so no blast damage is accounted for in the DPS

Heavy Weapons.name: Brisant (350m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Slug splitter shotgun dispersion becomes wildy variable and dependant on enemy hitbox size past 300m or so. Take this with a grain of salt.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Vortex
Medium Weapons.notes: Target stands still, assumes all missiles hit. This almost never happens, a ~55kph robot walking sideways will take 0 damage from these. Lockdown is strongly advised.

Medium Weapons.name: Murix (25m)
Medium Weapons.notes: Includes DoT damage, much of which is dealt while reloading. You'll only get this much damage at point blank. Reload starts 0.3s after mag empties, for some reason.

Medium Weapons.name: Kroko (Charged)
Medium Weapons.notes:
A full charge requires enemies to deal about 350k damage to you. Shields count. 
Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Cryo (Frozen)
Medium Weapons.notes: Range is 350m but projectile travel time signficiantly lowers DPS so point blank is assumed. Cryo weapons rarely actually hit their full DPS potential. Freeze damage boost accounted for.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Cryo (No Freeze)
Medium Weapons.notes: Range is 350m but projectile travel time signficiantly lowers DPS so point blank is assumed. Cryo weapons rarely actually hit their full DPS potential. Freeze damage boost NOT accounted for.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Shocktrain
Medium Weapons.notes: Assumes pilot with T4 powerman skill (-30% charge time)

Medium Weapons.name: UE Arm-M (No % Damage)
Medium Weapons.notes:
Percentage damage is widely variable since it's based on current health. Arms are MUCH better than they are shown here, but accurately estimating the percentage damage they deal is difficult.
Just keep in mind that a mag of 4 arms can easily do ~75% of an enemy's HP, no matter how well defended they are. The base DPS becomes relevant only when the enemy becomes so low on HP
that the percentage damage is irrelevant (or when shooting shields).

Medium Weapons.name: UE Venom
Medium Weapons.notes:
Damage is almost 100% DoT. This weapon cannot reliably break brittle aegis shields and all its damage can be healed. If you can solve these problems, however, it's an insane weapon.
Since the damage is DoT and the reload is insanely short, I calculated the DPS as though the DoT finishes ticking by the time its read to start shooting again. In reality, this doesn't happen, but 
there is no other way to represent the leftover DoT that I'm aware of. Treat this damage number as an average, because the leftover DoT means that the DPS ramps up over time (until the enemy dies).
The DoT damage IS AFFECTED BY WEAPON LEVEL. The stat screen makes it seem that past mk1.12, it no longer does more damage, but it does. Tested at mk2.12, like everything else on this sheet.
